metadata.name: Experiment 02 - Mistral Document AI on Azure Foundry
metadata.description: E02: Mistral Document AI accessed via the Azure AI Foundry serverless deployment (mistral-document-ai-2512), parallel to the public-API path. Sync OCR + document_annotation in a single HTTP call; same canonical post-processing chain (cleanup → checkConfidence) and HITL gate as the public-API Mistral template. Forks the Mistral provider to keep the public-API path intact for fallback comparison.

modelId.description: Foundry deployment id for Mistral Document AI; the activity falls back to this default when the document's stored model_id is not a Mistral deployment name.

templateModelId.description: Labeling TemplateModel id (documents table metadata.templateModelId overrides this when set). Mistral Document AI requests document_annotation from field_schema when non-empty. Default points at the seeded SDPR template that ships with the dataset.

confidenceThreshold.description: OCR confidence threshold for human review

ocrResponse.description: Raw Foundry OCR response, emitted alongside ocrResult so the benchmark sample workflow's persistOcrCache step can write it to benchmark_ocr_cache.

parameters.documentAnnotationPrompt:
You are extracting structured field values from a BC SDPR Monthly Report form.
Read the form image carefully and return values for every field in the schema.
Treat the printed form layout as the source of truth and prefer literal,
verbatim transcription over normalisation.

<form_layout>
The form has two parallel income columns in Section 2: APPLICANT (left) and
SPOUSE (right). Each `applicant_*` field reads the LEFT column ONLY; each
`spouse_*` field reads the RIGHT column ONLY. Never copy a value across
columns. Income rows are ordered: Net Employment Income, Employment Insurance,
Spousal Support / Alimony, Child Support, WorkBC Financial Support, Student
Funding, Rental Income, Room/Board Income, Worker's Compensation, Private
Pensions, OAS/GIS, Trust Income, Canada Pension Plan (CPP), Tax Credits,
Child Tax Benefits, Income Tax Refund, All Other Income, Income of Dependent
Children. Section 1 has Yes/No checkbox pairs for nine questions; questions
5-9 have separate Applicant and Spouse pairs (suffix `_spouse_yes` /
`_spouse_no`).
</form_layout>

<numeric_income_rules>
For every `applicant_*` and `spouse_*` numeric field in Section 2:

- If the cell is COMPLETELY EMPTY (no ink, no print, no mark of any kind in
  the cell), return `null`.
- If the cell visibly contains `0`, `$0`, `$ 0`, `0.00`, a written-out zero,
  a dash `-`, `nil`, `none`, or `N/A` written/printed inside it, return the
  number `0`. Handwritten zeros may look like `O`, a small loop, or `()` —
  treat those as `0` when they sit inside an income cell.
- Otherwise return the dollar amount as a plain number (no `$`, no commas,
  no spaces). `1,234.56` becomes `1234.56`.

Important: only return `0` when you can SEE a zero / dash / "N/A" mark
INSIDE the cell. Do NOT infer `0` from context — for example, do not assume
the spouse column is `0` just because the applicant column is. An empty
cell with no ink stays `null`. If you can see no marks in the entire spouse
column on this form (no spouse name, no spouse signature, no spouse
checkboxes filled), prefer `null` for every spouse_* income field.
</numeric_income_rules>

<checkbox_rules>
Checkbox fields end in `_yes` or `_no`. The form's checkbox style is a small
square ☐ that becomes filled (☑, ☒, X-mark, tick, scribble, dot inside) when
selected. Read each checkbox INDEPENDENTLY:

- If the box visibly contains ANY mark (X, ✓, scribble, blacked-out fill,
  dot), return `selected` for THAT field.
- If the box is empty/clean, return `unselected` for THAT field.

The Yes and No boxes are separate fields — do not assume "if YES is selected
then NO is unselected" without looking at NO. Some respondents check both,
some leave both blank, some check neither in error. Return what you literally
see in each box.

For questions 5-9 there are separate Applicant and Spouse rows. Read them as
independent pairs — Applicant's row drives `_yes` / `_no`, Spouse's row drives
`_spouse_yes` / `_spouse_no`.
</checkbox_rules>

<text_field_rules>
PRESERVE FORMAT. For every text field, return the value EXACTLY as written on
the form, character for character including:

- Spaces, including double spaces or unusual spacing.
- Punctuation: hyphens, parentheses, slashes, commas, periods.
- Capitalization (do not change case).
- Original separators in numbers (do NOT strip hyphens from `123-456-789`,
  do NOT add hyphens to `123456789`, do NOT change `2025-Nov-12` to ISO).

Specifically:

- `sin` / `spouse_sin`: the Social Insurance Number AS WRITTEN — preserve
  hyphens, spaces, or no separators based on what appears on the form.
- `phone` / `spouse_phone`: the telephone number AS WRITTEN — preserve
  parens, hyphens, dots, spaces, or whatever format appears.
- `date` / `spouse_date`: the date AS WRITTEN. If the form prints
  `2025-Nov-12`, return `2025-Nov-12`. If it prints `1985JAN4`, return
  `1985JAN4`. If it prints `2026-03-24`, return `2026-03-24`. Do NOT
  normalise to a different format.
- `name` / `spouse_name`: the printed full name AS WRITTEN, preserving
  spacing and capitalization. Distinct from `signature`.
- `signature` / `spouse_signature`: WHATEVER MARK is visible inside the
  signature box — cursive name, initials, single character (e.g. `X`),
  scribble, or short text. If the box is completely empty, return `""`.
  This is a recall-sensitive field: do not skip the box. If you see ANY ink
  inside, transcribe it.
- `explain_changes`: the free-text paragraph AT BOTTOM of the form, character
  for character including punctuation, spacing, capitalization, and
  abbreviations. Do not paraphrase, do not "clean up". `""` if blank.

Return `""` (empty string) ONLY for text fields where the field on the form
is genuinely blank (no ink inside the cell or signature box).
</text_field_rules>

<scope>
Officer-only / case-management fields (NEXT CHEQUE ISSUE, CASE ID, CASELOAD,
ADDRESS BLOCK, etc.) are NOT in the schema — ignore them. Only return values
for the field_keys provided in the schema.
</scope>

Be conservative on illegible text fields: return `""` only when the cell is
truly empty. If you can read SOMETHING inside the box (even partially),
return what you see.

fieldDescriptions.checkbox_need_assistance_yes: Section 1 Q1 'Are you still in need of assistance?' — APPLICANT-section YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_need_assistance_no: Section 1 Q1 'Are you still in need of assistance?' — APPLICANT-section NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_family_assets_yes: Section 1 Q2 'Has your family unit received or disposed of any assets?' — YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_family_assets_no: Section 1 Q2 'Has your family unit received or disposed of any assets?' — NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_shelter_yes: Section 1 Q3 'Any changes to your shelter costs?' — YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_shelter_no: Section 1 Q3 'Any changes to your shelter costs?' — NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_dependants_yes: Section 1 Q4 'Any changes in Dependants or Persons living in the home?' — YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_dependants_no: Section 1 Q4 'Any changes in Dependants or Persons living in the home?' — NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_employment_changes_yes: Section 1 Q5 'Any employment changes?' — APPLICANT row, YES box (left column).
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_employment_changes_no: Section 1 Q5 'Any employment changes?' — APPLICANT row, NO box (left column).
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_employment_changes_spouse_yes: Section 1 Q5 'Any employment changes?' — SPOUSE row, YES box (right column).
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_employment_changes_spouse_no: Section 1 Q5 'Any employment changes?' — SPOUSE row, NO box (right column).
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_school_yes: Section 1 Q6 'Are you attending / enrolled in school or training?' — APPLICANT row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_school_no: Section 1 Q6 'Are you attending / enrolled in school or training?' — APPLICANT row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_school_spouse_yes: Section 1 Q6 'Are you attending / enrolled in school or training?' — SPOUSE row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_school_spouse_no: Section 1 Q6 'Are you attending / enrolled in school or training?' — SPOUSE row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_work_yes: Section 1 Q7 'Are you looking for work?' — APPLICANT row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_work_no: Section 1 Q7 'Are you looking for work?' — APPLICANT row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_work_spouse_yes: Section 1 Q7 'Are you looking for work?' — SPOUSE row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_work_spouse_no: Section 1 Q7 'Are you looking for work?' — SPOUSE row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_moved_yes: Section 1 Q8 'Have you moved or entered a facility?' — APPLICANT row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_moved_no: Section 1 Q8 'Have you moved or entered a facility?' — APPLICANT row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_moved_spouse_yes: Section 1 Q8 'Have you moved or entered a facility?' — SPOUSE row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_moved_spouse_no: Section 1 Q8 'Have you moved or entered a facility?' — SPOUSE row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_warrant_yes: Section 1 Q9 'Any outstanding warrants for your arrest?' — APPLICANT row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_warrant_no: Section 1 Q9 'Any outstanding warrants for your arrest?' — APPLICANT row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_warrant_spouse_yes: Section 1 Q9 'Any outstanding warrants for your arrest?' — SPOUSE row, YES box.
fieldDescriptions.checkbox_warrant_spouse_no: Section 1 Q9 'Any outstanding warrants for your arrest?' — SPOUSE row, NO box.
fieldDescriptions.explain_changes: Free-text paragraph below 'Please explain all changes including income and submit proof:'. Verbatim transcription, exact punctuation/spacing/capitalization.
fieldDescriptions.signature: APPLICANT's signature box at the bottom — return any visible mark (cursive, initials, single character, scribble). Different field from `name` (printed). Empty string only if the box is blank.
fieldDescriptions.spouse_signature: SPOUSE's signature box at the bottom — return any visible mark. Empty string only if blank.
fieldDescriptions.date: APPLICANT's signature date in the box labelled 'Date (yyyy-mmm-dd)'. Preserve the exact format printed/written on the form.
fieldDescriptions.spouse_date: SPOUSE's signature date. Preserve the exact format printed/written on the form.
fieldDescriptions.name: APPLICANT's printed full name in the 'Applicant Print Name' box. Verbatim, preserve case and spacing.
fieldDescriptions.spouse_name: SPOUSE's printed full name in the 'Spouse Print Name' box. Verbatim, preserve case and spacing.
fieldDescriptions.phone: APPLICANT's telephone number in the 'Applicant Telephone' box. Preserve format exactly as written (parens, hyphens, dots, spaces).
fieldDescriptions.spouse_phone: SPOUSE's telephone number. Preserve format exactly as written.
fieldDescriptions.sin: APPLICANT's Social Insurance Number. Preserve format exactly as written (hyphens, spaces, or no separators).
fieldDescriptions.spouse_sin: SPOUSE's Social Insurance Number. Preserve format exactly as written.
fieldDescriptions.applicant_net_employment_income: Section 2, row 'Net Employment Income', APPLICANT (left) column.

ocr-extraction.label: OCR (Mistral on Foundry)
ocr-extraction.description: Mistral Document AI synchronous OCR via the Azure AI Foundry deployment

quality-gate.label: Quality Gate
quality-gate.description: Check OCR confidence and decide if human review is needed

human-review.label: Human Review
human-review.description: Wait for human approval when confidence is low
